title: Export to Excel (CSV)
excerpt: We get a lot of requests from our teams to have an option to export to an excel file without formatting. Having the groups on Monday & colors is great, but we need a way to quickly select which columns need to be exporte…

posts.author: MichaelCalvinJones (Michael Jones)
posts.cookedHtml: We get a lot of requests from our teams to have an option to export to an excel file without formatting. Having the groups on Monday & colors is great, but we need a way to quickly select which columns need to be exported and a way to export with CSV to eliminate formatting. It seems like a rather basic feature that is hard to believe it’s not possible. Is this on the roadmap anywhere or does anyone has a workaround that is quick and easy?

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @MichaelCalvinJones !

You can use this app

It solved the issue in this discussion

Hope this helps.

posts.author: MichaelCalvinJones (Michael Jones)
posts.cookedHtml: Appreciate you sending this through. We took a look at that application but it doesn’t feel scalable from a true enterprise standpoint. Is there a way to create that application within an actual view so you don’t need the link to directly access it? Similar to how doc-u-gen has their built out.

posts.author: Bianca (Bianca Clement)
posts.cookedHtml: Following- I would like to know if you find something that can do this.

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @MichaelCalvinJones & @Bianca

Just to confirm, you would like a Monday view with a simple button that exports the entire board to a CSV file (to download, not to link)?

posts.author: MichaelCalvinJones (Michael Jones)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, I think that would make it a lot easier to navigate. Additionally if it was possible to tell the application which columns to export instead of the entire board.

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @MichaelCalvinJones & @Bianca !

Try this app, it’ll give you an XSLX or CSV export, according to the columns you choose.
Just attach it as a view to the relevant board.

image1593×1137 78.7 KB

Hope this solves the problem

posts.author: MichaelCalvinJones (Michael Jones)
posts.cookedHtml: This is awesome! Great work, willing to throw you some payment for the efforts. Is it possible to have the item name column? Doesn’t appear we are getting that column option.

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
@MichaelCalvinJones !
I’m so glad you liked it!

The name column will appear by default, is that an issue? I can make it optional.

posts.author: jennap (Jenna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @MichaelCalvinJones !

It looks like Yoggi’s post here seems to have answered your question, is that correct?: Export to Excel (CSV) - #8 by Yoggie

If so, remember to mark it as the solution so others can easily find it as well and to close the thread as necessary Otherwise, feel free to ask any additional questions as needed!

Cheers,
Jenna

posts.author: mattwright (Matt Wright)
posts.cookedHtml: Love the export functionality, especially without the formatting. Is this export possible to automate for multiple boards? We have individual projects each on their own board. It’d be great to be able to automate all boards to export to excel instead of one-by-one. Is that possible?

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @mattwright !

Would you like all boards you choose to be consolidated into one file? or should it separate them into individual xlsx files?

posts.author: alexcamp (Alexandre Campagna)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 here for this request.
My solution @MichaelCalvinJones was to create a “master” excel file with all the correct formatting, also set as read only to prevent overwriting.
I take the exported excel from Monday, copy the data, and paste as “values only” into the master file.
I then “save as” on the disk.
Hope this helps ,

posts.author: mattwright (Matt Wright)
posts.cookedHtml: Combining multiple board data into one file would be better. Looking to create a single export of time and budget (across multiple boards) so that we can create pivots and analyze cumulative data.

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @mattwright !

Thank you!
I’m working on a new feature that does exactly that.
In the mean time though, you can check out a CSV URL app.
This solution might give you everything you need. It’ll link your google sheets to Monday boards and you can even set a function that refreshes the data every few hours or days.

Hope this solves the problem for now.

posts.author: MichaelCalvinJones (Michael Jones)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Yoggie,

This has been awesome! One question, we are getting a server error when other people try and use the app outside of myself. Any idea why this may be occurring? I’d like to make sure everyone on our accounts can have access to the tool. Thank you!

Best,

Michael

Screen Shot 2021-08-20 at 8.12.03 AM2236×1226 59 KB

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!

@alexcamp & @mattwright
I added a new feature.
You can now download multiple boards as a file, every board will be a different sheet inside it.

image1553×902 49.6 KB

@MichaelCalvinJones
I opened the app for multiple user access. Other users can access it now.
Note that they will only be able to generate files based on their Monday permissions.

Let me know if there are any issues.

posts.author: mattwright (Matt Wright)
posts.cookedHtml: Brilliant Yoggie! I’ll take a look this weekend. Thanks so much for sharing.

posts.author: MichaelCalvinJones (Michael Jones)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Yoggie,

It is telling me that the trial has expired, is there something we need to do to keep using the application? Thank you!

Best,

Michael

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @MichaelCalvinJones

Since the app is still in beta, I’ve reopened the access.
I’m glad to see you find the app useful, please let me know there are any issues.

posts.author: MichaelCalvinJones (Michael Jones)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you Yoggie, we are having an issue on a specific board where the application won’t load after we “login” See the screenshot. It just spins.

Screen Shot 2021-08-29 at 5.35.37 PM1568×955 39.5 KB

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just wanted to let you know of an alternative. The Analytics & Reports by Screenful can combine data from any number of monday boards into charts and task lists, and allows exporting them into CSV. You can freely pick the columns from one or more boards that you want to export.

Notice that you won’t be able to export directly from the board view. Instead, you can place Screenful charts into monday dashboard and export from there. You can learn more at

Screenful

Add Screenful charts and reports to Monday dashboard as widgets — Screenful

Start by creating a Monday dashboard if you haven't done that already. Add a widget to this dashboard by clicking the Add a widget button and selecting Apps as shown in the screenshot below.

posts.author: RyanSWS (Ryan Gottshall)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Yoggie! Came across your app and I was wondering if there is a way to access sub-item columns as part of the export to xlsx. When I try to use the app it is only showing the main columns as options for export. Thanks!

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @RyanSWS !

Unfortunately no, the subitem’s columns don’t always match the columns of the board itself, so an xls file can’t handle that.

I would love hearing about your use case so maybe I could find a better solution.
You can contact me here: yogev@formrally.com

posts.author: Clayton (Clayton Coetzee)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Yoggie
Ive been trialling your app and its awesome. Its really allowing my team to get some great data from Monday.

I wanted to ask you something, at the moment your current program does not export any data from a formula’s column. Is it possible to update that?

We have some information that gets pulled together in a formula but we are not able to see that in the CSV file because its the one thing that doesnt pull through.

Please let me know.

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Clayton & @RyanSWS @MichaelCalvinJones & @Bianca (@here)

I’ve added support for Formula columns in the Monday Reports app.
Also - you might want to check out the CSV-LINK app that also supports the formula columns.

Let me know if you need help with this.
Yogev
monday.formrally.com

posts.author: Emil (Emil)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Yoggie

Your app Monday reports seems to do everything we want it to do but what is stopping us from using it are all the permissions needed. We have sensitive data that has to be protected and giving anyone access to read/write all the data in our boards just seems too risky.

Have you overcome the same issues with other companies? If so, how did you do it?

Thanks.

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Emil !

I totally understand.
I’ve got clients that needs better security, heavier payloads, etc, so they cannot use the free version I’ve built.

I highly suggest building a dedicated app just for you.
If you’re interested I’m available at yogev@formrally.com

Cheers

posts.author: Hirof (Yolly)
posts.cookedHtml: All we have to do is click the 3 dot menu at the top right corner of our board, click “More actions” and then “Export board to Excel”. From here, we can choose whether we want to export just the table or to include all updates and update replies.

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Paul_K
Sorry I’m not at the community that often because Monday.com disabled many features (such as messaging).

You can contact me at:
yogev@formrally.com

posts.author: Mike_Larson (Mike Larson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Michael,

It seems that you just need to be able to export all the necessary columns to CSV. I think Skyvia can help you to do it easily. It was built for automation tasks (so you can schedule the export on a regular basis), however it also allows you to choose only needed columns for exporting.

The tool is freemium, and probably for this simple task the free plan will be enough for you or others who have the same task to do.

posts.author: xatxat (Simon Kusterer @ Gorilla Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone,

just wanted to let you know that you can also use our app Spreadsheet Gorilla to export one or more monday.com boards to CSV.

Here is a short explainer video:

If you have questions, just let me know.

– Simon

posts.author: Sogand_Okhovatian (sogand okhovatian)
posts.cookedHtml: Is it possible to automate exporting within Spreadsheet Gorilla? For instance an automation process that exports board data into CSV or excel files? I’ve seen a few apps that can automate exporting to google sheets but CSV or excel files are preferred.

posts.author: xatxat (Simon Kusterer @ Gorilla Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Not yet, but automation is one of the next things on the roadmap. The vision of Spreadsheet Gorilla is to have an All-in-one app when it comes to Spreadsheets + monday.com

Out of curiosity: which kind of automation would you be most eager to have? For example, send CSV/Excel as an E-Mail attachment?

posts.author: tself (Timothy Self)
posts.cookedHtml: My vote would be to have it automatically put in a google drive folder (or other such place like Dropbox or OneDrive) or update a Google sheet

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Sogand_Okhovatian @idoputer @Hirof @Paul_K

auth.monday.com

Welcome to monday.com | a new way of working

monday.com Work OS is an open platform where anyone can create the tools they need to run every aspect of their work.

Cheers.

posts.author: MASO (Markus @ Gorilla Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Sogand_Okhovatian

we have good news. Now a couple of months later, we finally shipped the Export boards to Excel with automations feature on Spreadsheet Gorilla. For now, it’s limited to Excel file attachments or embedded tables in emails. We will add an automation with CSV soon too.

Regarding the main topic of this thread, we released another handy app called Exported Excel Cleaner in the meantime. As @MichaelCalvinJones requested, the app lets you clean up your exported Excel files from monday.com with ease and convert it to CSV and other formats. Here is a short, self-explanatory demo video.

Let me know if you have any questions.

– Markus

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there,
There’s another approach to manage this.
You can auto-import your board into a Smart Spreadsheet app, hide or delete the columns you need, and then export a file into csv or spreadsheet file.
